commit.message:
refactor: decompose init.ts into modular src/commands/init/ directory

Split the 1214-line monolithic init.ts into 6 focused modules:
- constants.ts: provider definitions, agent lists, legacy key fields
- ui-helpers.ts: clearScreen, printHeader, formatChoice, printSummary
- wizard-steps.ts: steps 1-4 (agents, default, API keys, tools)
- wizard-steps-advanced.ts: steps 5-8 (labels, models, verification, retry)
- integrations.ts: GitHub/Linear/Jira/Slack/notifications/project settings
- index.ts: main init() orchestrator

All files under 300 lines. No functional changes.

.showFeaturePoints with professional outdoor-friendly visualization.

commit.message:
fix(backlog): resolve multi-select click after cross-table move (PUNT-358) (#429)

## Summary
- After multi-selecting tickets and moving them between backlog and
sprint (via drag or context menu), the selection state was not cleared,
causing subsequent single clicks on selected tickets to be ignored
(early return in click handler designed for drag-start purposes)
- Added `clearSelection()` calls after cross-table moves in 3 locations:
sprint-backlog-view drag handler, backlog page drag handler, and ticket
context menu (remove-from-sprint, add-to-sprint, send-to-list-position)
- Follows existing pattern: respects `keepSelectionAfterAction` user
preference, only clears for multi-select moves (single ticket moves are
unaffected)

## Test plan
- [x] Multi-select tickets in backlog, drag to sprint, then click one
ticket — drawer should open
- [ ] Multi-select tickets in sprint, drag to backlog, then click one
ticket — drawer should open
- [ ] Multi-select tickets, right-click > Move to Sprint, then click one
— drawer should open
- [ ] Multi-select tickets, right-click > Remove from Sprint, then click
one — drawer should open
- [ ] Multi-select tickets, right-click > Send to Top/Bottom of Sprint,
then click one — drawer should open
- [x] Verify single-ticket drag between tables still works correctly
- [ ] Verify `keepSelectionAfterAction` preference is respected
(selection preserved when enabled)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Secure token storage with httpOnly cookies and add PDF build dependencies (#122)

* feat: add auto-migration on Railway deploy

Migrations now run automatically before gunicorn starts.
Also added healthcheck endpoint config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: switch to Railpack builder with automatic migrations

- Change builder from NIXPACKS to RAILPACK in railway.json
- Add collectstatic to Procfile release phase (after migrate)
- Archive nixpacks.toml as .backup (no longer used)

Railpack will now execute migrations automatically before each deploy via Procfile release phase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Merge : Reverse Merge main into dev (#117)

* fix: correct Railway build paths to backend directory

Railway was looking for requirements.txt and manage.py at root, but they're in backend/.
This caused gunicorn to not be installed, resulting in "command not found" error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: railway settings

* fix: flatten requirements.txt for Railway deployment

Railway Nixpacks expects requirements.txt at project root, not a reference
to requirements/requirements.txt. Copied all dependencies directly to
backend/requirements.txt to resolve "gunicorn: command not found" error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove duplicate DATABASES config breaking Railway

Railway provides DATABASE_URL env var, but duplicate DATABASES block
(lines 194-210) required individual DATABASE_NAME/USER/PASSWORD vars.
Removed duplicate block - smart fallback logic (lines 96-127) handles
DATABASE_URL correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: switch to Railpack builder with automatic migrations (#118)

- Change builder from NIXPACKS to RAILPACK in railway.json
- Add collectstatic to Procfile release phase (after migrate)
- Archive nixpacks.toml as .backup (no longer used)

Railpack will now execute migrations automatically before each deploy via Procfile release phase.

* chore: add releaseCommand to Railway config for migrations

Railway needs releaseCommand to run python manage.py migrate before
container starts. With Railpack + backend root, the repo-root railway.json
wasn't being picked up, so DB migrations never ran.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use preDeployCommand instead of releaseCommand in Railway config

releaseCommand is not a valid field in Railway's railway.json.
The correct field name for Railpack is preDeployCommand, which runs
before the container starts. This ensures migrations run before
Django tries to serve requests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: railway json to collect statics

* Fix asynchrone request when registering in production

* test vercel config for 404 on refresh bug

* fix refreshToken bug that needed a valid access token that made logout impossible

* fix: Make logout idempotent by always returning 204 from the backend and consistently clearing local tokens on the frontend.

* fix various bugs: refresh token in storage, missing dependacy for pdf generation

* feat: surface email_verified in API and show verification banner

- Add email_verified field to UserViewModel DTO and propagate through

- Add VerificationBanner component in MainLayout shown when

- Update auth tests to reflect httpOnly cookie refresh token flow
- Fix reset-password route: /auth/reset-password → /reset-password

* fix: handle exceptions when sending password reset emails

* feat: implement Brevo API token sender for password reset emails and update settings

* fix: handle potential failure in token refresh process

* fix: update password reset flow with error handling and success message display

* test to trigger vercel push

* refactor: replace fetch with apiClient in resetPassword function and clean up error handling

* final commit

commit.message:
[chore] Add pre-commit hooks to dependabot (#14306)

## Describe your changes

See: https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/pull/14306#issue-4053683789

Adds pre-commit ecosystem to Dependabot configuration to automatically
keep pre-commit hook versions up-to-date.

- Adds `pre-commit` package ecosystem with weekly schedule
- Ignores `ruff-pre-commit` (synced separately via `sync-ruff-version`
hook)
- Uses 5-day cooldown consistent with other ecosystems

## Testing Plan

- [x] YAML syntax validated
- [ ] CI checks

| Type | Name | Count |
|------|------|------:|
| subagent | fixing-pr | 1 |
| subagent | general-purpose | 1 |

feat: two-step pipeline — classification + dedicated fiscal review

Step 1 (Haiku): classifies category, account, CdC, article only
Step 2 (Haiku): reviews fiscal treatment with dedicated thinking budget
- Deducibility, IVA, ritenuta, reverse charge, bene strumentale
- Conservative defaults when uncertain
- Generates interactive fiscal alerts for the AI assistant box

Benefits:
- Each step has focused thinking → no more shortcuts
- Fixes: "bene strumentale" on leasing installments
- Fixes: inconsistent 20%/100% across batches
- Cost: +$0.01/invoice for the fiscal pass

commit.message:
v1.1: Fix Steam ID format and decrypt detection

- MandarinJuice uses Steam64 IDs, not Steam32. Auto-convert input.
- Detect successful decrypt via stdout ("Decrypted the") instead of
  relying only on _OUTPUT directory which may not be created.
- Deduplicate IDs (Steam32+Steam64 pairs resolve to same Steam64).
- Show both Steam32 and Steam64 in logs and info.txt.
- Add console output messages for success/failure.

commit.message:
Day 5b: add log snapshot compaction and multi-key 2PC transactions

- node_raft_sharded.py: log snapshot compaction (SNAPSHOT_THRESHOLD=20),
  absolute log index tracking (log_offset), /install_snapshot endpoint for
  lagging followers, ThreadingHTTPServer to fix self-RPC deadlock
- node_raft_sharded.py: multi-key transactions via two-phase commit
  (/txn, /txn_prepare, /txn_commit, /txn_abort), per-key locking with
  10s timeout and background cleanup loop
- test_raft_sharded.py: automated test suite (31 cases) covering basic
  reads/writes, leader forwarding, snapshot generation and recovery,
  txn commit, lock conflicts, and lock timeout auto-release
- README.md: Day 5b section with feature descriptions and new API table
- .gitignore: ignore snapshot_*.json

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(infra): Kubernetes local development infrastructure (#6)

* feat(infra): US-001 - Initialize Pulumi cluster project

Add Pulumi project for Kubernetes cluster provisioning with:
- Project config (Pulumi.yaml) with tdp-cluster name and nodejs runtime
- Local stack config (Pulumi.local.yaml) with k3d cluster type
- TypeScript strict config and package.json with pulumi dependencies
- Dispatcher pattern in index.ts routing on clusterType config
- k3d provider module using @pulumi/command

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update progress after US-001 completion

* feat(infra): US-002 - Implement k3d cluster provider

Create Pulumi cluster project (US-001 scaffold) and implement k3d
provider that creates a local Kubernetes cluster with:
- Local container registry via k3d registry create
- Port mappings for Traefik ingress (80, 443)
- Configurable worker node count via Pulumi config (default: 2)
- Kubeconfig exported as a Pulumi output
- Destroy/cleanup commands for cluster and registry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(infra): add package-lock.json for cluster project

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update progress after US-002 completion

* feat(infra): US-003 - Initialize Pulumi platform project

Add tdp-platform Pulumi project that reads kubeconfig from the cluster
stack via StackReference and creates a k8s.Provider for downstream use.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update progress after US-003 completion

* feat(infra): US-004 - Verify Traefik ingress and configure platform layer

Add the platform layer (Layer 2) Pulumi project that bootstraps core
services onto any cluster provisioned by the cluster layer:

- Document that Traefik is bundled with k3s/k3d (no separate install)
  with examples of Ingress and IngressRoute resource definitions
- Install cert-manager via Helm with installCRDs: true and
  environment-appropriate resource limits
- Install kube-prometheus-stack via Helm with environment-appropriate
  resource limits, retention, and scrape intervals
- Platform index.ts imports chart modules and exports k8sProvider

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(infra): add platform package-lock.json for reproducible installs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update progress after US-004 completion

* feat(infra): US-005 - Create ServiceDeployment component resource

Add reusable Pulumi ComponentResource that encapsulates a Kubernetes
Deployment + Service + optional Ingress with consistent
app.kubernetes.io/* labels across all resources.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update progress after US-005 completion

* feat(infra): US-006 - Create local-up and local-down scripts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update progress after US-006 completion

* feat(infra): US-007 - Add smoke test for local cluster

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update progress after US-007 (needs human review)

* feat(infra): US-008 - Implement Linode k3s cluster provider

Add a Linode k3s provider that provisions a Linode 2GB instance with k3s
installed via the official installer. Includes firewall rules for ports
80, 443, and 6443, and exports kubeconfig as a Pulumi output. Updates
index.ts to dispatch to the new provider when clusterType is linode-k3s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update progress after US-007, US-008

* feat(infra): US-009 - Create base Dockerfile and example service image

Add a multi-stage Python base Dockerfile (Dockerfile.base) and an example
FastAPI service demonstrating how to extend it. Both images build
successfully with docker. The services README documents the build and
k3d registry push workflow.

commit.message:
fix: count all completed contributions when validating jar break

The break guard was only counting isSettled=true contributions, but
the app's visible balance includes completed-but-not-yet-settled
contributions. This let users break a jar with a visible balance > 0
since the hook saw 0. Now counts all completed contributions.

commit.message:
Fix backspace showing ^? by restoring scroll region reset for input

The previous fix kept the scroll region locked during input(), which
broke terminal line editing (backspace, arrows) since the cursor was
outside the scroll region. Now we reset the region for input() (so
line editing works) but clean up scroll_end after Enter (which the
old code missed), preventing bar duplication.

fix: resolve YAML syntax error in CI pipeline that broke all workflow runs

The notify-status job had a backtick template literal with ${{ }} expressions
inside a YAML script block that the parser interpreted as nested mappings.
This has caused every CI run to fail since the workflow was created. Rewrote
the PR comment script to avoid the YAML parsing conflict and removed the
unconfigured email notification step.

commit.message:
fix: remove invalid AUTO_HTTPS directive from Caddyfile

Caddy's auto_https only accepts disable values (off, disable_redirects,
etc.), not "on". Use SITE_ADDRESS=:80 instead to disable auto-HTTPS
when behind a reverse proxy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove dead .optional() after .default() on agentTimeout and commandTimeout

The `.default().optional()` chain made `.default()` dead code — `.optional()`
intercepts undefined before the default can apply. Removed `.optional()` so
the type is always `number` with a guaranteed default. Simplified consumer
ternaries in runner.ts accordingly.

counters tightened, origin story spacer reduced, bottom nav clearance padding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Bold full-page layout, Modern bg-photo visibility, logo filter

3 critical bugs fixed:

1. Bold title slide only showing quarter-page: removed errant
   `position:relative` that overrode `position:absolute;inset:0`
   from the base slide system

2. Modern preset hid background photos: `>*{z-index:1}` selectors
   were catching `.bg-overlay` div, overriding its `position:absolute`
   and collapsing it to zero height. Changed to `>*:not(.bg-overlay)`
   in Modern, Classic, and Bold presets

3. Bold logo invisible: `brightness(0) invert(1)` destroyed colored
   logos. Changed to `brightness(1.8) saturate(0)` — brightens and
   desaturates to near-white without destroying the image

Also raised default background opacity from 8% to 15% so images are
actually visible out of the box.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Consolidate stock update into single SureDone request

Replaced three separate SureDone API calls (stock update, channel
push, auto-relist) with one combined request containing stock,
shelf, condition, channel flags, and googleskip=1 to skip broken
Google Shopping channel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: revert extractCity to index-based for 2+ candidates

The regex approach broke street names without standard suffixes (e.g.
"De Hoge Vlucht") — they were returned as city instead of being skipped.
City is always the second meaningful segment in Dutch/Nominatim addresses,
so use candidates[1] when 2+ candidates remain. The looksLikeStreet check
is now only used for the 1-candidate fallback to avoid returning a bare
street name when no city is present.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix all clippy warnings workspace-wide and add grep performance guard

- Fix ~25 clippy errors across 12 crates (collapsible_if, ToString→Display,
  map_or→is_some_and, derivable_impls, &PathBuf→&Path, wildcard patterns,
  double_ended_iterator_last, Default::default, redundant closures)
- Add grep_guard.rs: detects dangerous recursive grep patterns that cause
  30+ minute hangs (missing --exclude-dir, binary scanning, no --include)
- Wire grep guard into pre_tool_bash gate with context injection
- Introduce UpsertParams struct in kavach-db to fix too_many_arguments
- 253 tests pass, 0 clippy warnings, 4.3MB release binary

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: copy @opentelemetry/api to root node_modules for Vercel bundler

Vercel's Next.js bundler resolves from the repo root (/vercel/path0/)
but npm install runs in pipeline-management/. Copy the transitive
@opentelemetry/api package to root so the bundler can find it.
